title: botrychium multifidum subsp. coulteri
intro: Botrychium multifidum subsp. coulteri, also known as Coulter's rattlesnake fern, is a subspecies of fern that belongs to the genus Botrychium. This plant is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Coulter's rattlesnake fern include reducing pain and swelling, treating wounds, and soothing digestive issues, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, or used topically in the form of salves or poultices.
